The customer journey is made up of several interactions a consumer has with a brand.   Effective touch points are designed to meet customer needs, spark positive emotions, and align seamlessly with their expectations. That's why your strategy should be carefully planned from the get-go! 🏃  These touch points include: 1️⃣ Pre-purchase: ad, social media, and promos 2️⃣ Time of purchase: landing pages, product packaging, and brick and mortar vists 3️⃣ Post-purchase: thank you emails, follow-ups, newsletters  Check out our blog to see how you can create effective touchpoints that resonate with your audience 👉 https://hubs.ly/Q03dkVv80  {hashtag|\#|ManagedMarketing} {hashtag|\#|Marketing} {hashtag|\#|CustomerJourney}

🔎 We’ve done the research—here are 6 marketing trends that will shape the future. If you’re in marketing, you NEED to know these.   Check them out in more detail in our blog!

If your #socialmedia strategy is just 'posting to post' - we need to talk. 😬⁠ ⁠ Often, we see brands treat social media like a bulletin board instead of two-way conversation. Who likes being talked AT? Rather, you need to have a strategy that intertwines engagement with authenticity, while also promoting your brand. ⁠ ⁠ That's why brands who focus on engagement over promotion, like @Duolingo and @HubSpot, see the most success! 🎉⁠ ⁠ Watch as we go over some key strategies every business should use, then head to our bio to learn more! ⤴️⁠ ⁠ #MarketingTips #SocialMediaTips

Social media marketing isn't just about posting - it's about connecting. ❤️  The best social media strategies don't just push promotions; they take time to build relationships with their audience. Customers want to engage with real people, not a business. So how do you strike the right balance? Brands that do it well see higher engagement, stronger loyalty, and more impact (no pun intended). Data lake or data warehouse? The way your business stores data impacts everything from analysis to decision-making. But how do you know which one is right for you? 🤔  A data lake stores massive amounts of raw, unstructured data—perfect for businesses collecting data at scale for deep analysis later.  A data warehouse, on the other hand, organizes structured data for quick access, making it ideal for reporting and analytics.   Choosing the right solution depends on your needs. Do you require flexibility and scale, or structured data for fast insights?   Check out our article where we dive into the differences and figure out which one works best for your company! 👇   {hashtag|\#|DataPrivacy} {hashtag|\#|DataSafety} {hashtag|\#|CyberTips} {hashtag|\#|Cybersec}

Starting this year, #CMMC 2.0 will be required for businesses working with the Department of Defense. Not complying could cost your business up to $1M per violation – definitely not how you want to kick off 2025. 😬   Whether you work with the DoD or just want to strengthen your security practices, CMMC compliance is your shield against #cyberthreats and costly risks.

Starting this year, {hashtag|\#|CMMC} 2.0 will be required for businesses working with the Department of Defense. Not complying could cost your business up to $1M per violation – definitely not how you want to kick off 2025. 😬   The updated Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification \(CMMC 2.0\) makes compliance simpler by reducing levels from five to three and offering smaller businesses the option to self-assess. It’s easier, more accessible, and still ensures strong protection for sensitive defense data. 🛡️✨   Whether you work with the DoD or just want to strengthen your security practices, CMMC compliance is your shield against cyber threats and costly risks.
